text.content:
Where the gorge river finally throws itself off the highland, it falls in a long white roar into a churning pool, and around that pool clings the trading village of Pacuare Falls. Spray drifts over its roofs day and night. This is a working place: boats from above are unloaded at the head of the cataract, cargo is carried down the slick portage stair, and reloaded onto lowland craft below. Everything that moves through the gorge passes across these docks, and the village lives on the toll of handling it.

It is busier and harder-edged than Our Pacuare — warehouses, a tavern, a factor who keeps the ledgers, porters for hire, and travelers passing through with stories from down-country. On a good year it hums.

This is not a good year. The portage has become a place where things vanish, the factor's prices have turned strange, and the porters speak carefully, with their eyes down. Whatever has its grip on the gorge, it has its grip tightest here.

The bandits' grip

Pacuare Falls is where the adventure comes to a head. The bandits work the portage around the falls — the one stretch every shipment must cross on foot — skimming cargo and intimidating the porters. Someone in the village is either cooperating with them or too frightened to resist: the factor who keeps the trade ledgers is the natural candidate (coerced, complicit, or simply paying protection). Decide which as the table's investigation unfolds.

Beats

  • Arrival contrasts sharply with Our Pacuare — wary villagers, inflated prices, a tavern where talk dies when strangers enter.
  • The portage stair and the pool below the falls are strong encounter sites for a 1st-level fight or chase.
  • The bandit camp lies just off the portage; resolving it reopens the river and restores trade (and the Keep's gathering yield upstream).

Replace the placeholder background with a village/falls map when art is ready; this scene links here via its Journal Entry field.
